title: Design System: Sotto il Cofano degli LLM

display.purpose: Cover title only — the one place the deck raises its voice.

headline.purpose: Per-slide h2 — the argument of the slide.

lead.purpose: Opening statement under a headline; light weight, ink-dark.

label.purpose: JetBrains Mono for machine artifacts — counts, tags, tokens, code.

shadows.name: accent-glow
shadows.purpose: Primary button and active nav arrow only — the violet halo on the live action.

motion.name: ease
motion.purpose: Default ease-out for slide transitions, hover lifts, reveals.

motion.name: slide-change
motion.purpose: Slide enter/exit: fade + 60px translateX + slight scale.

motion.name: stagger-rise
motion.purpose: Staggered entrance of a slide's direct children.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary action — violet→teal gradient with accent glow. Rare; the deck is keyboard-driven.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.98rem; padding: 12px 24px; border-radius: 10px; border: none; cursor: pointer; background: linear-gradient(120deg, #6c5ce7 0%, #0a9d92 100%); color: #fff; box-shadow: 0 14px 36px -10px rgba(108,92,231,0.40); transition: transform 0.2s, box-shadow 0.2s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 18px 40px -10px rgba(108,92,231,0.5); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #6c5ce7; outline-offset: 3px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.4; transform: none; box-shadow: none; cursor: default; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary action — surface-tint fill, hairline border.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.98rem; padding: 12px 24px; border-radius: 10px; cursor: pointer; background: rgba(20,22,40,0.035); color: #1b1d2b; border: 1px solid rgba(20,22,40,0.12); transition: transform 0.2s, border-color 0.2s; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); border-color: #6c5ce7; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #6c5ce7; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Concept Card
components.description: Soft-lifted card with figure icon, title, body; rises and borders violet on hover.
components.html:
Scopo

Comprendere a fondo il funzionamento interno, non solo l'output.
components.css: .ds-card { background: linear-gradient(180deg, #ffffff, rgba(20,22,40,0.035)); border: 1px solid rgba(20,22,40,0.12); border-radius: 18px; padding: 26px; box-shadow: 0 2px 6px rgba(20,22,40,0.05), 0 1px 2px rgba(20,22,40,0.04); transition: transform 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), border-color 0.3s, box-shadow 0.3s; max-width: 320px; } .ds-card:hover { transform: translateY(-6px); border-color: #6c5ce7; box-shadow: 0 26px 60px -16px rgba(20,22,40,0.20), 0 6px 16px rgba(20,22,40,0.07); } .ds-card-icon { margin-bottom: 14px; } .ds-card-title { font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 1.18rem; font-weight: 700; color: #1b1d2b; margin: 0 0 8px; } .ds-card-body { font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 0.98rem; line-height: 1.6; color: #4d5165; margin: 0; }

components.name: Kicker Chip
components.description: Pill label above a heading — teal mono-ish label in a tinted pill. (Numbered variant is slop debt; prefer a plain named kicker.)
components.css: .ds-kicker { display: inline-block; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.14em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #0a9d92; padding: 6px 14px; border: 1px solid rgba(20,22,40,0.12); border-radius: 999px; background: rgba(20,22,40,0.035); }

components.name: Nav Arrow
components.description: Fixed circular glass nav button; fills violet with glow on hover.
components.css: .ds-nav-arrow { width: 52px; height: 52px; border-radius: 50%; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.6); -webkit-backdrop-filter: saturate(150%) blur(14px); backdrop-filter: saturate(150%) blur(14px); border: 1px solid rgba(20,22,40,0.12); color: #1b1d2b; font-size: 1.8rem; line-height: 1; cursor: pointer; display: grid; place-items: center; box-shadow: 0 10px 30px -8px rgba(20,22,40,0.12), 0 2px 6px rgba(20,22,40,0.05); transition: all 0.25s cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-nav-arrow:hover { background: #6c5ce7; color: #fff; border-color: #6c5ce7; transform: scale(1.08); box-shadow: 0 14px 36px -10px rgba(108,92,231,0.40); } .ds-nav-arrow:disabled { opacity: 0.2; cursor: default; }

components.name: HUD Counter
components.description: Top-right slide position — mono counter in a glass pill, violet current number.
components.css: .ds-hud { display: inline-flex; gap: 14px; align-items: center; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 0.82rem; } .ds-hud-section { color: #4d5165; letter-spacing: 0.05em; } .ds-hud-counter { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; color: #898ea3; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.55); -webkit-backdrop-filter: saturate(150%) blur(14px); backdrop-filter: saturate(150%) blur(14px); border: 1px solid rgba(20,22,40,0.12); padding: 4px 12px; border-radius: 999px; box-shadow: 0 2px 6px rgba(20,22,40,0.05); } .ds-now { color: #0a9d92; }

narrative.overview: A technical deck that explains a machine by drawing it. Findings are laid out with engineering clarity on a cool, near-white page; ink-dark text carries the prose, a monospace voice marks the technical asides, and every slide resolves to one figure that makes a single mechanism visible. The lineage is Distill.pub and 3Blue1Brown crossed with Stripe/Linear docs. It is shown projected to a room of technical peers, so it reads from the back row. The personality is precise, lucid, unshowy: depth comes from sequence, one idea per slide, and color is used as signal, not decoration. Surfaces are soft-lifted — a quiet shadow at rest, a gentle rise on hover.
keyCharacteristics: Cool near-white page (#f6f7fb), ink-dark text, projection-legible contrast.
One accent as signal (violet primary), one counter-voice (teal); magenta/red reserved for warnings.
Inter across the whole hierarchy, JetBrains Mono for the technical/label voice.
Soft-lifted surfaces: quiet shadow at rest, rise on hover.

rules.name: The Two-Voice Rule
rules.body: Diagrams speak in exactly two hues — Signal Violet and Diagram Teal — to separate the two sides of an idea. A third color in a figure must mean a third thing (usually Warning Red for failure), or it doesn't belong.

rules.name: The Signal-Not-Decoration Rule
rules.body: Color carries meaning. If a fill, glow, or accent isn't distinguishing one concept from another, it's decoration — remove it or make it neutral.

rules.name: The One Display Rule
rules.body: The 900-weight display scale appears on the cover and nowhere else. Inside the deck, hierarchy is carried by the headline/lead/body steps.

rules.name: The Mono-Is-Technical Rule
rules.body: JetBrains Mono means 'this is a machine artifact' — a count, a tag, a token, code. Never use it for prose just for flavor.

rules.name: The Rise-On-Hover Rule
rules.body: Interactive surfaces translate up (−6px cards, −2px buttons) and deepen their shadow on hover. The motion and the shadow change together; one without the other looks broken.

dos: Do lead each slide with one figure that makes a single mechanism visible; let prose annotate the figure, never the reverse.
Do keep the two-voice color discipline in diagrams: Signal Violet (#6c5ce7) and Diagram Teal (#0a9d92), with a third hue only when it means a third thing.
Do keep contrast projection-safe: ink (#1b1d2b) / ink-dim (#4d5165) on the cool page; never drop body copy to ink-faint (#898ea3).
Do reserve the 900-weight display scale for the cover; carry in-deck hierarchy with headline/lead/body.
Do use JetBrains Mono only for machine artifacts — counts, tags, tokens, code.
Do pair the −6px/−2px hover rise with a shadow change; reserve the violet accent glow for the primary action and active nav.
donts: Don't ship generic corporate-PowerPoint moves — bullet-point walls, clip-art, stock-photo gradients, template chrome.

Don't fall into AI-slop SaaS landing tells: gradient text via background-clip:text, identical icon-card grids, tiny tracked uppercase eyebrows above every slide, or decorative glassmorphism. The current deck still carries some of these — treat them as debt to retire, not patterns to extend.
Don't overstimulate: no motion for its own sake, no neon, no animation that competes with the content.
Don't go childish — no cartoonish illustration, bubbly rounded everything, or emoji.
Don't add color that isn't signal. If a fill or glow doesn't distinguish one concept from another, make it neutral or remove it.
